introSection: Screamin' Delta Demon was a steel bobsled roller coaster at Opryland USA in Nashville, Tennessee, USA. It was manufactured by Intamin.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Screamin' Delta Demon was built at a cost of $3.7 million and opened in June 1984.

After the closure of Opryland USA in 1997, most of the rides, including Screamin' Delta Demon, were purchased by Six Flags (then Premier Parks) and placed at the site of Old Indiana Fun-n-Water Park in Thorntown, Indiana, USA, a property Six Flags planned to develop. In July 2002, the property was sold off with most of the rides scrapped and removed from the site in 2003, but a deal allowed Six Flags to keep Chaos and Screamin' Delta Demon on the property until 2006 so that Six Flags would relocate them to other properties.

By June 2006, Screamin' Delta Demon was removed from the site and was assumely ultimately scrapped.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The ride had the same layout as La Vibora and Alpine Bobsled.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: White track with a green outside and supports.

sections.title: External links
sections.content: * Screamin' Delta Demon on the parks website

fullText:
Screamin' Delta Demon was a steel bobsled roller coaster at Opryland USA in Nashville, Tennessee, USA. It was manufactured by Intamin.

Screamin' Delta Demon was built at a cost of $3.7 million and opened in June 1984.

After the closure of Opryland USA in 1997, most of the rides, including Screamin' Delta Demon, were purchased by Six Flags (then Premier Parks) and placed at the site of Old Indiana Fun-n-Water Park in Thorntown, Indiana, USA, a property Six Flags planned to develop. In July 2002, the property was sold off with most of the rides scrapped and removed from the site in 2003, but a deal allowed Six Flags to keep Chaos and Screamin' Delta Demon on the property until 2006 so that Six Flags would relocate them to other properties.

By June 2006, Screamin' Delta Demon was removed from the site and was assumely ultimately scrapped.

The ride had the same layout as La Vibora and Alpine Bobsled.

White track with a green outside and supports.
